revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Iida Kofun group]][[ja:飯田古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Iida Kofun cluster | native_name =飯田古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = Iinuma Unzaiji Kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption =Iinuma Unzaiji Kofun | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Iida Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|30|08|N|137|50|43|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|qid=Q841129|lt=Iida, Nagano|1=Iida, Nagano}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th to late 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Iida Kofun cluster'''|飯田古墳群|IIda Kofun-gun}} is group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the Shimoida neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Iida, Nagano|en|Iida, Nagano|qid=Q841129|lt=Iida, Nagano|1=Iida, Nagano}} in the {{ill|Chūbu region|en|Chūbu region|qid=Q134638|lt=Chūbu region|1=Chūbu region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Thirteen tumuli have been collectively protected as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] since 2016.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/221444|title=飯田古墳群|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}</ref>
==Outline== In the Shimoina area of southern Nagano prefecture is a group of ancient Kofuns distributed on {{ill|Fluvial terrace|en|Fluvial terrace|qid=Q1069618|lt=river terraces|1=Fluvial terrace}} of the right bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. There were once more than 520 ''kofun'' in Iida, of which a total of 22 are collectively called the "Iida Kofun cluster". These tumuli consist of 18 keyhole-shaped ({{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}) and four scallop-shaped {{nihongo|''hotatekaigata-kofun''|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun}} tumuli. The presence of so many of this style of tumuli point to a very strong political and cultural connection to the ancient {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|qid=Q2658252|lt=Yamato kingdom|1=Yamato Kingship}}. The Iida tumulus group is presumed to have been built in the middle-late Kofun period in the latter half of the 5th century and the end of the 6th century. During this period, it is postulated that the Iida area was a major horse breeding base for the Yamato kingdom, and the introduction of horses led to the development of inland transportation networks and inter-regional trade, which is reflected in various aspects of each tumulus. Of the group, 11 keyhole-shaped and two scallop-shaped tumulus received National Historic Site designation in 2016.
The largest of these tumuli is the {{nihongo|'''Unsaiji Kofun'''|雲彩寺古墳|Unsaiji kofun}}, also known as the Iinuma Tenjindsuka Kofun, which is a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a total length of 74.5 meters. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a lateral type stone corridor with an entrance to the west, with the total length of 13 meters. The entrance is blocked, but the back wall on the east side has been removed. The length of the chamber itself is 5.7 meters by 2.3 meters, with a height of 1.7 meters. It was opened in the Edo period, at which time {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, beads, rings, horse harnesses, and pottery was excavated, but most of these artifacts have been lost. The neighboring Unsai-ji temple, whose main hall partially encroaches on the tumulus, has preserved a golden ring and two bells from a horse harness. The tumulus was built in the middleof the 6th century. It was designated a Nagano Prefectural Historic Site in 1965.

9.41 | E |display=inline}}||scallop||45m||vertical ||late 5c|| |- | Tatsuoka || Osarudo Kofun || Kiribayashi || {{coord|35 | 27 | 41.76 | N | 137 | 48 | 44.56 | E |display=inline}}||keyhole||65.4m|| horizontal ||mid 6c|| bronze mirror (ICP) found |- | Tatsuoka || Masedsuka Kofun || Kamikawaji || {{coord|35 | 27 | 48.68 | N | 137 | 48 | 51.50 | E |display=inline}}||keyhole||46.4m|| horizontal ||late 6c|| last constructed |- |}
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== * [https://www.city.iida.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/2017011901.html Iida City official website] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.iida-museum.org/kouko/lib/new_page_22.htm List of kofun in the vicinity of Iida], which also includes kofun not belonging to the cluster on the official website of Iida City Museum (in Japanese) *Tourist guide training for the [https://msnav.com/7758 Iida Kofun cluster] (in Japanese) *Guided tours of the [https://www.mstb.jp/news/limited/561/ Iida Kofun cluster] (in Japanese) Iida Kofun group
